title: POP: a high information density and extendable DNA database
author: Cao, Rangli
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: DNA has been widely identified as the most promising storage medium because of its desirable storage performance. However, this medium stays within the conceptual framework for a long time, subject to the “write once, read all” storage model. We construct a “plasmid-of-pool”(POP) to actualize an extensible DNA digital library and data management. The application of a POP-based database overcomes the limitations of “one pool storage”. Firstly, we developed a set of coding rules based on the modulation process. The coding density exceeds 1.7 bits/nt, and the base sequences have optimal biocompatibility. Next, we established the logical layer of the DNA database. In this procedure, defined primer sequences function as address sequences. We tested the data rewrite and random access performance of the database by a specific polymerase chain reaction. Compared with the ssDNA pool, POP improved the theoretical capacity and expansibility of DNA database.
